name: Auburn City Park
description: Auburn City Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Auburn, MI. There are 3 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free.

name: Bay County Community Center
description: Bay County Community Center is a place to play pickleball in Bay City, MI. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — $5.00 Drop in fee / $25 for the month

name: Carroll Park
description:
Wind nets surround the (6) courts, surfaces are in nice condition no serious cracks.
Its rumored six more courts are coming.
Busy days such as Fridays and Saturdays 8am to 12pm see upwards of 30 to 40 players.
A paddle placement rack is used to organize the next four players in when a court opens up.
Its a mixed bag on skill levels, weekday AM players are typically in their 60s, and 70s for the most part very friendly and very competitive.
Wiz

name: Dow Bay Area Family Y
description: Dow Bay Area Family Y is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Bay City, MI. There are 2 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play.

name: Kawkawlin Township Park
description: Kawkawlin Township Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Kawkawlin, MI. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free.
